monday.description: Build clean coordination between your fretting and picking hands. Speed comes from accuracy and relaxation, not force.

exercises.name: Ascending Chromatic Motif
exercises.tips: Keep both hands as relaxed as possible and monitor your forearms for unnecessary tension. Use the minimum amount of finger pressure required to sound each note cleanly. If tension appears, slow the tempo and rebuild accuracy before increasing speed.

exercises.name: Asymmetrical Chromatic Grouping
exercises.tips: Maintain strict alternate picking even as the accents shift against the beat. Do not allow the accent pattern to alter your picking mechanics. Focus on rhythmic consistency rather than speed.

exercises.name: Burst Then Relax Run
exercises.description:
Alternate slow notes with fast bursts.

```
e|----------------5-6-7-8-|
B|------------------------|
   s s s s f f f f
```
exercises.tips: Treat the fast notes as short, controlled bursts rather than sustained speed. Actively release tension immediately after each burst. The goal is to teach your hands how fast playing should feel without fatigue.

exercises.name: Single-String Speed Cycling
exercises.description:
1-2-3-4 pattern on a single string.

```
e|-5-6-7-8-5-6-7-8-|
```
exercises.tips: Focus on even pick attack and consistent timing between notes. Because string crossings are removed, inefficiencies in your picking motion will be more obvious. Keep movements small and controlled.

tuesday.description: Improve accuracy and control when moving across non-adjacent strings.

exercises.name: Skipped Triad Shapes
exercises.tips: Pick deliberately and avoid brushing adjacent strings. Ensure unused strings are muted at all times. Accuracy and clean silence are more important than tempo.

exercises.name: Inside–Outside Skip Alternation
exercises.tips: Be consciously aware of whether each string change is inside or outside picking. Do not compensate with sweeping or economy picking. Keep the pick path intentional and consistent.

exercises.name: Muted Skip Accuracy Drill
exercises.description:
Muted fretting hand, focus on pick travel.

```
e|--x-----x-----x--|
B|------x-----x----|
G|x-----x----------|
```
exercises.tips: Lightly mute the strings with your fretting hand so pitch is removed from the exercise. Listen closely for missed strings or unintended contact. This drill isolates picking accuracy without distraction.

exercises.name: Single-Note Skip Ladder
exercises.tips: Move the pick deliberately between strings rather than relying on momentum. Prepare the next string crossing mentally before executing it. Keep the motion calm and controlled.

wednesday.description: Develop strict alternate picking with challenging crossings.

exercises.name: Scalar Alternate Line
exercises.tips: Maintain down-up consistency at all times, even when it feels inefficient. Avoid the temptation to use economy picking on string changes. Clarity and rhythmic precision are the primary goals.

exercises.name: String-Crossing Displacement Run
exercises.tips: Allow mistakes to reveal weaknesses rather than correcting them with technique shortcuts. Keep the picking hand motion consistent across all groupings. Slow down if necessary to preserve form.

exercises.name: Two-String Crossing Loop
exercises.description:
Short loop across two strings.

```
e|--5-7-5-7-|
B|--5-7-5-7-|
```
exercises.tips: Focus on how the pick escapes the string after each stroke. Keep the loop smooth and even, without accenting string changes unintentionally. Small motions are critical here.

exercises.name: Accent Displacement Alternate Line
exercises.tips: Separate rhythmic accents from picking mechanics. The pick should move identically on accented and unaccented notes. Accents come from controlled emphasis, not extra force.

thursday.description: Improve reach and independence. Comfort always comes first.

exercises.name: Four-Fret Expansion
exercises.tips: Stop immediately if you feel pain or sharp discomfort. Keep the thumb relaxed behind the neck to reduce strain. This exercise should feel challenging but controlled.

exercises.name: Held-Anchor Stretch Line
exercises.tips: Ensure the anchored finger remains planted throughout the entire pattern. Move the other fingers slowly and deliberately. Avoid twisting the wrist to reach notes.

exercises.name: Spider Stretch (1-3-2-4)
exercises.tips: Maintain a neutral wrist angle and curved fingers. Focus on finger independence rather than speed. This drill should feel mentally engaging but physically light.

exercises.name: Micro-Shift Stretch
exercises.description:
Shift up one fret each cycle.

```
e|--5---8--6---9--7---10-|
```
exercises.tips: Treat each shift as a fresh position rather than a continuation of the stretch. Keep movements slow and intentional. Do not rush through uncomfortable ranges.

exercises.name: Hammer-On / Pull-Off Ladder
exercises.tips: Aim for equal volume between picked and legato notes. Use finger strength rather than extra pick attack. Keep unused fingers relaxed and close to the fretboard.

exercises.name: Offset Legato Accents
exercises.tips: Generate accents using finger pressure and timing, not picking. Maintain smooth legato motion even as accents move. Avoid tightening the hand to emphasize notes.

exercises.name: Descending Pull-Off Drill
exercises.tips: Pull off with intention so each note rings clearly. Do not rely on gravity or finger collapse. This drill exposes and strengthens weaker fingers.

exercises.name: Held-Note Legato Independence
exercises.description:
One finger holds while others move.

```
e|--5h7h8--|
B|--5------|
```
exercises.tips: Keep the held note completely stable while other fingers move. Avoid sympathetic finger motion. This drill builds true finger independence and control.

saturday.description: Train clean landings and confident shifts.

exercises.name: Position Jump Motifs
exercises.tips: Visualize the target fret before moving your hand. Land directly on the note without sliding. Accuracy should feel decisive, not tentative.

exercises.name: Blind Position Targeting
exercises.description:
Look away after first position.

```
e|--5-----------12--|
```
exercises.tips: Trust spatial awareness rather than visual confirmation. Move confidently and commit to the landing. Avoid micro-adjustments after contact.

exercises.name: Octave Jump Targeting
exercises.tips: Mentally map the octave shape before jumping. Keep the hand relaxed during the shift. Clean landings matter more than speed.

exercises.name: Delayed Landing Drill
exercises.tips: Use the pause to clearly visualize the target position. Move only after you are confident in the landing. This trains intention rather than reaction.

sunday.description: Smooth chord changes and continuous motion.

exercises.name: Triad Flow
exercises.tips: Lift fingers only as much as necessary between shapes. Allow transitions to overlap slightly for smoothness. Avoid stopping the sound between chords.

exercises.name: Voice-Led Triad Transitions
exercises.tips: Focus your attention on the moving voice rather than the full chord shape. Keep stationary notes firmly in place. Smoothness is more important than tempo.

exercises.name: Single-Finger Pivot Changes
exercises.tips: Use the anchored finger as a physical reference point. Let the other fingers move efficiently around it. Avoid lifting the pivot finger unnecessarily.

exercises.name: Rhythmic Delay Transitions
exercises.tips: Stay relaxed even as the change is delayed. Keep the rhythm steady and controlled. This drill builds confidence during imperfect transitions.
